incidents.vector: Phishing campaign delivered through the Signal messaging app to top German decision-makers, attempting to harvest credentials or session data.
incidents.impact: Berlin says affected officials were notified and the data leak has been stopped, but it cannot rule out that other targets were compromised. Authorities suspect Russian state actors.

incidents.vector: Email-flooding followed by Microsoft Teams messages from attackers posing as IT help desk; victims are tricked into installing a malicious browser extension (SnowBelt) and follow-on tools (SnowGlaze, SnowBasin) that act as a backdoor and credential harvester.
incidents.impact: Mandiant says the UNC6692 cluster is using the access to maintain persistent control of corporate accounts and move laterally inside victim environments without re-authenticating.

incidents.vector: Voice-phishing and fake single sign-on pages run by the BlackFile / Cordial Spider group (linked to The Com); operators impersonate IT support, harvest credentials, escalate into executive accounts, then exfiltrate data from Microsoft Graph, Salesforce, SharePoint and internal repositories.
incidents.impact: Unit 42 and RH-ISAC report seven-figure ransom demands, a dedicated leak site, and physical 'swatting' of executives to pressure payment. Activity has been continuous since February 2026.

incidents.victim: Navigate360 (P3 Global Intel school tip line)
incidents.vector: Exploitation of platform vulnerabilities in the P3 Global Intel anonymous school-safety tip line.
incidents.impact: Hackers claim to have exfiltrated 93 GB of data, including identifying information on students who reported tips. Senators Hassan and Banks have demanded answers; Navigate360 supports more than 30,000 schools and 5,000 public-safety agencies.

incidents.vector: Spamouflage influence network used 90+ inauthentic Facebook accounts and 13 Instagram profiles, plus AI-generated imagery, to push narratives attacking incumbent leader Penpa Tsering and the legitimacy of the parliamentary vote.
incidents.impact: DFRLab found the activity attracted virtually no organic engagement, but the operation extends a multi-year Spamouflage targeting of Tibetan, Taiwanese, US and Japanese audiences.

incidents.victim: npm developer ecosystem (Bitwarden, Checkmarx, Trivy, LiteLLM, Telnyx, Axios users)
incidents.vector: TeamPCP used stolen developer tokens to publish trojanized packages — most recently @bitwarden/cli 2026.4.0 — that download a Bun-based payload to harvest npm tokens, GitHub PATs, AWS/Azure/GCP credentials, and AI API keys, then republish themselves to spread.
incidents.impact: The campaign is the latest wave of the Shai-Hulud worm pattern; Unit 42 and Recorded Future warn of cascading compromises across multiple security vendors and CI/CD pipelines, with TeamPCP claiming 300+ GB of stolen data and possible ties to Lapsus$.

incidents.victim: Cisco ASA / Firepower Threat Defense customers (incl. US federal agencies)
incidents.vector: UAT-4356 (the ArcaneDoor actor) chained CVE-2025-20333 and CVE-2025-20362 in Cisco ASA WebVPN to deploy the FIRESTARTER backdoor inside the LINA process, achieving persistence by abusing the Cisco Service Platform mount list (CSP_MOUNT_LIST).
incidents.impact: CISA confirmed FIRESTARTER survives firmware patching on compromised devices; Emergency Directive 25-03 was updated to require federal agencies to collect forensic data and apply new vendor updates. Joint advisory issued with NCSC-UK, ACSC and partners.

incidents.vector: Intrusion into Vercel's internal systems followed by exfiltration of customer data; ShinyHunters publicly claimed responsibility on a leak forum.
incidents.impact: Vercel confirmed the breach, notified a small number of affected customers, and recommended that all customers rotate keys and secrets; subsequent reporting indicates fallout has expanded to additional customers and third-party systems.

incidents.vector: Unspecified intrusion that forced the city to take its IT network offline; surrounding Leon County disconnected its network as a containment measure, language consistent with a ransomware response.
incidents.impact: City websites and several public services remained unavailable for days. No ransomware crew has publicly taken credit yet.

incidents.vector: Cyber Av3ngers (CL-STA-1128 / Storm-0784) shifted from Unitronics PLCs to Rockwell Automation FactoryTalk and Allen-Bradley PLCs, installing FactoryTalk on VPS infrastructure to support reconnaissance of internet-exposed ICS devices.
incidents.impact: Unit 42 observed roughly 5,600 Rockwell/Allen-Bradley devices exposed globally; CISA issued an advisory on April 7 mirroring the findings. Activity coincides with renewed Iranian cyber escalation around the US-Israel Iran conflict.

incidents.victim: Ukrainian prosecutors and EU militaries (Greece, Romania, Serbia)
incidents.vector: GRU-linked operators broke into the email accounts of more than 170 Ukrainian prosecutors and corresponding ministries in three EU countries, seeking access to investigative material and military correspondence.
incidents.impact: Reuters reports the campaign pursued sensitive case files on Russian war crimes; the same activity also touched military targets in Greece, Romania, and Serbia.

incidents.vector: Theft of customer data from McGraw Hill's Salesforce environment, consistent with the broader ShinyHunters Salesforce-targeting campaign.
incidents.impact: Personal information for 13.5 million users — including names, addresses, emails, and phone numbers — was leaked after a failed extortion attempt.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of a smart-contract vulnerability allowing the attacker to withdraw assets as rsETH (restaked Ether) tokens.
incidents.impact: Roughly $292 million in crypto assets stolen — the largest crypto-heist of 2026 to date.

incidents.vector: Intrusion into an internal Standard Bank network used to store customer data; specific attack vector not disclosed.
incidents.impact: Standard Bank — South Africa's largest bank — confirmed the breach. It is the third disclosed hack against a South African bank this year, suggesting a coordinated campaign against the sector.
Disruption to systems supporting customers in Europe and Australia; Autovista provides pricing and residual-value data widely used by insurers and OEMs.

incidents.vector: Pre-staged manipulation of margin-trading logic via fake token pools created over several days, then exploitation of vulnerabilities in the protocol's margin-trading feature.
incidents.impact: Approximately $18.4 million in crypto assets stolen.

incidents.vector: Automated scanning and exploitation of CVE-2023-33538 in TP-Link TL-WR940N, TL-WR740N, and TL-WR841N routers, dropping a Mirai-like ARM ELF binary (Condi variant) that contacts a hard-coded C2 at 51.38.137.113 to recruit devices into a DDoS botnet.
incidents.impact: Unit 42 observed sustained large-scale exploitation since the CVE was added to the CISA KEV catalog in June 2025; affected routers are end-of-life and will not receive vendor patches, leaving widespread devices recruitable as long as default credentials persist.

incidents.vector: Pro-Russian hacktivist intrusion into the OT environment of a thermal power plant in western Sweden, attempting destructive action.
incidents.impact: Sweden's minister of civil defense disclosed the attack publicly in April 2026; the plant's built-in safeguards caught the intrusion before any disruption occurred. Swedish officials linked the group to Russia's intelligence and security services.
